text:
CT CHEST, ABDOMEN, AND PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION: 58-year-old male with newly diagnosed sigmoid colon adenocarcinoma on colonoscopic biopsy. Staging evaluation. Known KRAS G12D mutation, MSS phenotype. Evaluate for locoregional and distant disease extent.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed using 2.5 mm axial reconstructions following administration of 100 mL of iohexol intravenous contrast material. Portal venous phase abdominal and pelvic imaging was obtained. Lung windows, mediastinal windows, and bone windows reviewed. Multiplanar reformats (coronal and sagittal) reviewed. Comparison: No prior cross-sectional imaging available.

---

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and airways: The lungs are clear bilaterally. No pulmonary nodules, masses, or consolidations identified. No pleural effusions. The central airways are patent. The trachea is midline. No endobronchial lesions. There is mild bilateral lower lobe dependent atelectasis, likely positional.

Mediastinum: The heart is normal in size. No pericardial effusion. The aorta and great vessels are normal in caliber. No mediastinal, hilar, or axillary lymphadenopathy by size criteria. No anterior mediastinal mass. The thymus is not enlarged.

Chest wall and pleura: Intact. No rib lesions. No pleural thickening or loculated collections.

---

ABDOMEN:

Liver: The liver is normal in size with smooth contour. Four discrete hypodense lesions are identified throughout the hepatic parenchyma:

- Segment VI (right posterior inferior): A well-defined, rounded hypodense lesion measuring approximately 3.1 x 2.8 cm in axial dimensions. The lesion demonstrates peripheral rim enhancement on arterial phase with central wash-out on delayed portal venous imaging, a pattern highly suggestive of metastatic deposit in this clinical context. No internal hemorrhage or calcification.

- Segment IVb (medial left lobe, inferior): A hypodense rounded lesion measuring approximately 2.2 x 1.9 cm, with similar enhancement characteristics to the segment VI lesion. Situated in proximity to the medial left hepatic lobe, adjacent to the falciform ligament insertion, with no involvement of hepatic vascular structures on this imaging.

- Segment II (lateral left lobe, superior): A smaller hypodense lesion measuring approximately 1.4 x 1.2 cm, with faint peripheral rim enhancement. No adjacent vascular or biliary involvement.

- Segment VII (right posterior superior): A small hypodense focus measuring approximately 0.9 x 0.8 cm, at the limits of confident characterization but with enhancement characteristics and morphology consistent with the other hepatic lesions and with metastatic involvement in this clinical context.

No evidence of biliary ductal dilatation. Common bile duct is not dilated (3 mm). No intrahepatic or extrahepatic biliary obstruction. Portal vein is patent with normal hepatopetal flow appearance.

Gallbladder: Normal in appearance. No gallstones. No wall thickening or pericholecystic fluid.

Spleen: Normal in size and attenuation. No focal splenic lesions.

Pancreas: Normal morphology. No pancreatic ductal dilatation. No peripancreatic fat stranding or fluid collections. No pancreatic mass identified.

Adrenal glands: Both adrenal glands are normal in size and configuration. No adrenal masses or thickening.

Kidneys: Both kidneys are normal in size with symmetric enhancement and excretion. A tiny 3 mm hypodense focus in the right renal cortex is too small to characterize but most likely represents a simple cyst. No hydronephrosis bilaterally. No renal masses.

Retroperitoneum and mesentery: No retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymphadenopathy by size criteria. No free retroperitoneal fluid or gas. No aortic aneurysm. The inferior vena cava is patent.

---

PELVIS:

Colon and rectum: There is a heterogeneously enhancing, irregular soft tissue mass arising from the sigmoid colon measuring approximately 4.8 x 3.9 cm in cross-sectional dimensions. The mass causes partial luminal narrowing of the sigmoid; however, the colon is not obstructed and there is no upstream colonic distension. Stranding of the adjacent pericolic fat is present, compatible with transmural extension of the primary tumor. No perforation. No abscess. The rectum is unremarkable.

Peritoneum: No peritoneal nodularity, omental caking, or ascites identified. The peritoneal surfaces appear smooth throughout the visualized abdomen and pelvis. No peritoneal implants identified.

Pelvic lymph nodes: A few pericolic lymph nodes are identified in the sigmoid mesentery, the largest measuring approximately 8 mm in short-axis dimension. Several small pelvic side wall lymph nodes are noted, none of which exceed 10 mm in short axis; these are borderline by size criteria only and may be reactive.

Bladder: Normal in appearance. No wall thickening or intraluminal lesions. The bladder is partially distended.

Prostate: Unremarkable in size and morphology for patient age. No discrete prostatic nodule on this study.

Seminal vesicles: Symmetric and unremarkable.

Bony pelvis: No destructive or sclerotic osseous lesions. No suspicious pelvic bony abnormality.

---

BONES AND SOFT TISSUES:

Visualized osseous structures: No suspicious lytic or sclerotic lesions in the visualized vertebral bodies, ribs, or pelvis to suggest skeletal metastatic involvement. Mild degenerative changes at the lower lumbar spine, including facet joint arthrosis at L4-L5 and L5-S1, in keeping with age-related change. No pathologic compression fracture.

Soft tissues: No subcutaneous masses or abnormal soft tissue collections. No abdominal wall hernia.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. Heterogeneously enhancing sigmoid colon mass measuring 4.8 x 3.9 cm with pericolic fat stranding, consistent with the known primary sigmoid colon adenocarcinoma. The mass causes partial luminal narrowing without evidence of obstruction. Findings are compatible with transmural tumor extension.

2. Four hepatic lesions with imaging characteristics consistent with synchronous metastatic deposits, distributed across segments VI (3.1 cm), IVb (2.2 cm), II (1.4 cm), and VII (0.9 cm). The lesions are multifocal and bilobar in distribution. There is no biliary obstruction and no involvement of major hepatic vascular structures on this examination.

3. No evidence of peritoneal carcinomatosis. The peritoneal surfaces are smooth without nodularity, omental deposits, or ascites.

4. No pulmonary nodules, masses, or pleural disease to suggest intrathoracic metastatic involvement at this time.

5. No mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy.

6. No evidence of osseous metastatic disease on this examination.

7. A few pericolic and pelvic sidewall lymph nodes are noted; these are borderline by size criteria only and may represent regional lymphadenopathy versus reactive nodes -- clinical and pathologic correlation advised.

Overall, the imaging findings are consistent with newly diagnosed colorectal carcinoma of the sigmoid colon with synchronous bilobar hepatic metastatic disease, without evidence of pulmonary, peritoneal, or osseous involvement.
